content.text:
```mdl
@ Contract: Two‑Way Confidentiality and Non‑Disclosure Agreement
module contract_nli_no_align_dev_3_nda_18

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Types
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Parties (Clause 1)
type Party = {
    name: string,
    address: string,
    tax_id: string,
    legal_capacity: string
}

@ Representatives (Clause 2)
type Representative = {
    name: string,
    party: Party                # the party the representative belongs to
}

@ Confidential information (Clause 1)
type ConfidentialInformation = {
    field: string,              # e.g. “technical data”, “business plan”
    description: string,
    is_disclosed: bool,         # true when the information has been disclosed
    consent_given: bool         # true when the disclosing party has given written consent
    is_returned_or_destroyed: bool   # true when the information has been returned or destroyed
}

@ Permitted purpose (Clause 1)
type PermittedPurpose = {
    description: string
}

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Entities
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ The two parties to the agreement (Clause 1)
entity disclosing_party: Party
entity receiving_party: Party

@ A representative of the disclosing party (Clause 2)
entity disclosing_rep: Representative

@ The confidential information that is exchanged (Clause 1)
entity confidential_info: ConfidentialInformation

@ The purpose for which the receiving party may use the information (Clause 1)
entity permitted_purpose: PermittedPurpose

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Helper functions
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Checks whether a party is allowed to disclose the information
func may_disclose(party: Party, info: ConfidentialInformation) -> bool:
    info.consent_given

@ Checks whether a party is allowed to use the information for a purpose
func may_use_for(party: Party, info: ConfidentialInformation, purpose: PermittedPurpose) -> bool:
    true   # the model assumes the purpose is always permitted when the clause is satisfied

@ Checks whether a party solicits a representative (hypothesis)
func solicits(party: Party, rep: Representative) -> bool:
    false   # placeholder – the rule will forbid any solicitation

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  Rules
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
@ Clause 3 – General confidentiality obligation
rule O keep_confidential:
    not confidential_info.is_disclosed or confidential_info.consent_given always

@ Clause 3  – No disclosure without prior written consent
rule F disclose_without_consent:
    confidential_info.is_disclosed and not confidential_info.consent_given always

@ Clause 3c – No use outside the permitted purpose
rule F use_outside_permitted_purpose:
    not may_use_for(receiving_party, confidential_info, permitted_purpose) always

@ Clause 3a – Take reasonable measures to protect the information
rule O reasonable_measures:
    true always   # abstract – the concrete measures are outside the logical model

@ Clause 5 – Return or destroy information at termination (or within 7 days of instruction)
rule O return_or_destroy_at_termination:
    confidential_info.is_returned_or_destroyed always

@ Clause 5 – Retain one copy only for regulatory/insurance purposes
rule O retain_one_copy_for_regulatory:
    (confidential_info.is_returned_or_destroyed or confidential_info.is_disclosed) always

@ Clause 4 – Access limited to representatives who need to know
rule O limited_access_to_representatives:
    true always   # abstract – the “need‑to‑know” condition is captured by the model’s structure

@ Clause 4 – Receiving party responsible for its representatives’ compliance
rule O representative_compliance:
    true always   # abstract – enforcement is outside the logical model

@ Hypothesis – Receiving Party shall not solicit any of Disclosing Party’s representatives
rule F solicit_disclosing_representatives:
    solicits(receiving_party, disclosing_rep) always

# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
#  End of model
# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
```
